description_long: This is a cylindrical stone core, likely extracted from a larger rock formation or concrete structure, with a rough, fractured surface and visible aggregate inclusions. The stone is predominantly gray, with some lighter and darker patches, and features several prominent cracks and chips, especially along one side where the internal aggregate is exposed. The ends of the cylinder are relatively flat but show signs of wear and minor chipping, with one end marked by faint, hand-etched writing. The surface texture is matte and uneven, with a hard, gritty feel typical of concrete or natural stone. The object appears dense and solid, with no visible cavities or openings, and its size suggests it could be handled by one or two people. This type of stone core is commonly found in urban construction sites, used for material testing or as a remnant from drilling operations.
description: A rough, gray cylindrical stone core with visible cracks, exposed aggregate, and a matte, gritty surface. One end is marked with faint writing, and the object shows signs of chipping and wear.
description_view_0: The end face of the cylinder is visible, showing faint writing and minor chipping around the edge.
description_view_1: The side view highlights a large fractured area with exposed aggregate and several surface cracks.
description_view_2: The opposite end face is mostly intact, with a few cracks and a smoother appearance compared to the other end.
description_view_3: This side view shows a long, continuous crack and a chipped section at the base, with less exposed aggregate than the other side.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or core sample on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular cracking and exposed aggregate, with faint hand-etched writing on one end; no regular or decorative patterns.
attribute_weathering: Cracked and chipped with rough, matte surfaces, indicating moderate weathering and handling; some aggregate exposure suggests mechanical damage.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts with smoother, man-made surfaces but may blend with other construction debris or core samples; typically rests on bare ground or concrete.
